mentions.note: Traditional enterprise software facing pressure from AI-powered alternatives; pricing power under threat as customers explore cheaper custom AI solutions.
Software/financial services company down almost 7% despite tax season strength; caught in broad software selloff.
Down 5% on AI displacement fears despite tax season support; stock experiencing relentless selling despite not expensive valuation.
Down 35% Q1 but up 8% since CEO Goodarzi spoke Feb 26; Cramer willing to stick neck out: AI platforms lack Intuit's consumer/SMB brand trust and expert network; moat durable.

Down 40% YTD (worst S&P 500 stock), but reported strong quarter: 17% revenue growth, margin beat, bottom-line beat. Mid-market up 40%, TurboTax up 12% despite IRS returns down 5%. AI-human hybrid model protects against SaaS apocalypse fears. Cramer sees dislocation opportunity.

Down 40% YTD before today's bounce; exceptional quarter (17% revenue growth, big bottom-line beat); management didn't raise full-year guidance (standard practice before tax season) and Q1 guidance light; stock trading lower AH but fundamentals strong.
Tax software platform eviscerated past 6 months on AI tax-planning disruption fears; real threat from Alteryx/Claude but market may be front-running impact.
TurboTax and QuickBooks; down 45%, 19x 2026 earnings; AI threat minimal for consumer tax product, SMB can't afford internal development; baby thrown out with bathwater; buy right now.
Software provider hammered down 11% on AI threat to tax and financial software; multiple compression risk remains.
Down 25% in January despite profitable model; multiple compression from 30x to 20x forward earnings; AI displacement fears; overshooting but risky.
